text: The surgeon inserts a reusable D-port into the anus and uses curved reusable instruments to perform a transanal laparoscopic repair. The procedure begins with lavage of the cavity and exploration of the lower rectum, revealing a large defect between twelve and three o'clock, connecting the lower rectum and the vagina.

The surgeon inserts a Vicryl 2-0 suture through the central port orifice, using a monocurve needle holder at the three o'clock position and monocurve grasping forceps at the nine o'clock position. Figure-of-eight sutures are applied to close the defect, first passing through the inferior edge and then the superior edge of the defect, which measures approximately two centimeters and is located twelve centimeters from the anal margin. An intraluminal knotting technique is employed to secure the sutures.

The surgeon operates using curved instruments to avoid clashing, cutting the suture with monocurved scissors and removing it through the port at the nine o'clock position. Additional sutures are placed to close a defect at the superior edge. Methylene blue is injected through the vagina, confirming no passage into the lower rectum, indicating successful closure.
